children.heading: Board
children.label: 1 Board
children.text: The term “Board” means the Board of Directors described in subsection (b)(2)(A).

children.heading: Department
children.label: 2 Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Energy.

children.heading: Executive Director
children.label: 3 Executive Director
children.text: The term “Executive Director” means the Executive Director described in subsection (b)(5)(A).

children.heading: Foundation
children.label: 4 Foundation
children.text: The term “Foundation” means the Foundation for Energy Security and Innovation established under subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Historically Black college or university
children.label: 5 Historically Black college or university
children.text: The term “historically Black college or university” has the meaning given the term “part B institution” in .section 1061 of title 20

children.heading: Individual Laboratory-Associated Foundation
children.label: 6 Individual Laboratory-Associated Foundation
children.text: The term “Individual Laboratory-Associated Foundation” means a Laboratory Foundation established by an operating contractor of a National Laboratory.

children.heading: Minority-serving institution
children.label: 7 Minority-serving institution
children.text: The term “minority serving institution” means a Hispanic-serving institution as defined in , an Alaska Native-serving institution and a Native Hawaiian-serving institution as defined in section in 1059d of title 20, or a Predominantly Black Institution, Asian American and Native American Pacific Islander-serving institution, or a Native American-serving nontribal institution as defined in .section 1101a of title 201section 1067q of title 20So in original. The word “in” probably should not appear.1

children.heading: National Laboratory
children.label: 8 National Laboratory
children.text: The term “National Laboratory” has the meaning given the term in .section 15801 of this title

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 9 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Energy.

children.heading: Tribal College or University
children.label: 10 Tribal College or University
children.text: The term “Tribal College or University” has the meaning given in .section 1059c of title 20

children.heading: Foundation for Energy Security and Innovation
children.label: b Foundation for Energy Security and Innovation

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall establish a nonprofit corporation to be known as the “Foundation for Energy Security and Innovation”.August 9, 2022

children.label: i
children.text: to support the mission of the Department; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to advance collaboration with energy researchers, institutions of higher education, industry, and nonprofit and philanthropic organizations to accelerate the commercialization of energy technologies.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: C Limitation
children.text: The Foundation shall not be an agency or instrumentality of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Tax-exempt status
children.label: D Tax-exempt status
children.text: The Board shall take all necessary and appropriate steps to ensure that the Foundation is an organization that is described in and exempt from taxation under .section 501(c) of title 26section 501(a) of title 26

children.heading: Collaboration with existing organizations
children.label: E Collaboration with existing organizations
children.text: The Secretary may collaborate with 1 or more organizations to establish the Foundation and carry out the activities of the Foundation.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: The Foundation shall be governed by a Board of Directors.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Board shall be composed of the ex officio nonvoting members described in clause (ii) and the appointed voting members described in clause (iii).

children.label: I
children.text: The Secretary.

children.label: II
children.text: The Under Secretary for Science.

children.label: III
children.text: The Under Secretary for Nuclear Security.

children.label: IV
children.text: The Chief Commercialization Officer.

children.label: aa
children.text: seek to enter into an agreement with the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine to develop a list of individuals to serve as members of the Board who are well-qualified and will meet the requirements of subclauses (II) and (III); and

children.label: bb
children.text: appoint the initial members of the Board from that list, if applicable, in consultation with the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine.

children.heading: Representation
children.label: II Representation
children.text: The appointed members of the Board shall reflect a broad cross-section of stakeholders from academia, National Laboratories, industry, nonprofit organizations, State or local governments, the investment community, and the philanthropic community.

children.label: AA
children.text: has experience in the energy sector;

children.label: BB
children.text: has research experience in the energy field; or

children.label: CC
children.text: has experience in technology commercialization or foundation operations; and

children.label: bb
children.text: to the extent practicable, represents diverse regions, sectors, and communities.

children.label: I
children.text: an individual to serve as Chair of the Board; and

children.label: II
children.text: an individual to serve as Vice Chair of the Board.

children.label: I
children.text: the date that is 3 years after the date on which the Chair or Vice Chair of the Board, as applicable, is designated for the position; and

children.label: II
children.text: the last day of the term of service of the member, as determined under subparagraph (D)(i), who is designated to be Chair or Vice Chair of the Board, as applicable.

children.label: I
children.text: shall not be representatives of the same area of subject matter expertise, or entity, as applicable, under subparagraph (B)(iii)(II); and

children.label: II
children.text: shall not be representatives of any area of subject matter expertise, or entity, as applicable, represented by the immediately preceding Chair and Vice Chair of the Board.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The term of service of each appointed member of the Board shall be not more than 5 years.

children.heading: Initial appointed members
children.label: II Initial appointed members
children.text: Of the initial members of the Board appointed under subparagraph (B)(iii)(I), half of the members shall serve for 4 years and half of the members shall serve for 5 years, as determined by the Chair of the Board.

children.label: I
children.text: shall be filled in accordance with the bylaws of the Foundation by an individual capable of representing the same area or entity, as applicable, as represented by the vacating board member under subparagraph (B)(iii)(II);

children.label: II
children.text: shall not affect the power of the remaining appointed members to execute the duties of the Board; and

children.label: III
children.text: shall be filled by an individual selected by the Board.

children.heading: Initial meeting
children.label: i Initial meeting
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the Board is established, the Secretary shall convene a meeting of the ex officio and appointed members of the Board to incorporate the Foundation.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: ii Quorum
children.text: A majority of the appointed members of the Board shall constitute a quorum for purposes of conducting the business of the Board.

children.label: i
children.text: establish bylaws for the Foundation in accordance with subparagraph (G);

children.label: ii
children.text: provide overall direction for the activities of the Foundation and establish priority activities;

children.label: iii
children.text: carry out any other necessary activities of the Foundation;

children.label: iv
children.text: evaluate the performance of the Executive Director; and

children.label: v
children.text: actively solicit and accept funds, gifts, grants, devises, or bequests of real or personal property to the Foundation, including from private entities.

children.label: I
children.text: policies for the selection of Board members, officers, employees, agents, and contractors of the Foundation;

children.label: aa
children.text: the acceptance, solicitation, and disposition of donations and grants to the Foundation, including appropriate limits on the ability of donors to designate, by stipulation or restriction, the use or recipient of donated funds; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the disposition of assets of the Foundation;

children.label: III
children.text: policies that subject all employees, fellows, trainees, and other agents of the Foundation (including ex officio and appointed members of the Board) to conflict of interest standards; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the specific duties of the Executive Director.

children.label: I
children.text: reflect unfavorably on the ability of the Foundation to carry out activities in a fair and objective manner; or

children.label: II
children.text: compromise, or appear to compromise, the integrity of any governmental agency or program, or any officer or employee employed by, or involved in, a governmental agency or program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: No member of the Board shall receive compensation for serving on the Board.

children.heading: Certain expenses
children.label: ii Certain expenses
children.text: In accordance with the bylaws of the Foundation, members of the Board may be reimbursed for travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, and other necessary expenses incurred in carrying out the duties of the Board.

children.heading: Restriction on membership
children.label: I Restriction on membership
children.text: No employee of the Department shall be appointed as a member of the Board of Directors.

children.label: A
children.text: to support the Department in carrying out the mission of the Department to ensure the security and prosperity of the United States by addressing energy and environmental challenges through transformative science and technology solutions; and

children.label: i
children.text: fostering collaboration and partnerships with researchers from the Federal Government, State governments, institutions of higher education, including historically Black colleges or universities, Tribal Colleges or Universities, and minority-serving institutions, federally funded research and development centers, industry, and nonprofit organizations for the research, development, or commercialization of transformative energy and associated technologies;

children.label: ii
children.text: strengthening and sharing best practices relating to regional economic development through scientific and energy innovation, including in partnership with an Individual Laboratory-Associated Foundation;

children.label: iii
children.text: promoting new product development that supports job creation;

children.label: I
children.text: to accelerate private sector competition and investment; and

children.label: II
children.text: that complement the use of prize authority by the Department;

children.label: v
children.text: supporting programs that advance technology maturation, especially where there may be gaps in Federal or private funding in advancing a technology to deployment or commercialization from the prototype stage to a commercial stage;

children.label: vi
children.text: supporting efforts to broaden participation in energy technology development among individuals from historically underrepresented groups or regions; and

children.label: vii
children.text: facilitating access to Department facilities, equipment, and expertise to assist in tackling national challenges.

children.heading: Studies, competitions, and projects
children.label: A Studies, competitions, and projects
children.text: The Foundation may conduct and support studies, competitions, projects, and other activities that further the purposes of the Foundation described in paragraph (3).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Foundation may award fellowships and grants for activities relating to research, development, demonstration, maturation, or commercialization of energy and other Department-supported technologies.

children.heading: Form of award
children.label: ii Form of award
children.text: A fellowship or grant under clause (i) may consist of a stipend, health insurance benefits, funds for travel, and funds for other appropriate expenses.

children.label: I
children.text: shall make the selection based on the technical and commercialization merits of the proposed project of the potential recipient; and

children.label: II
children.text: may consult with a potential recipient regarding the ability of the potential recipient to carry out various projects that would further the purposes of the Foundation described in paragraph (3).

children.heading: National Laboratories
children.label: iv National Laboratories
children.text: A National Laboratory that applies for or accepts an award under clause (i) shall not be considered to be engaging in a competitive process.

children.label: i
children.text: to leverage the capabilities and facilities of National Laboratories to commercialize technology; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to assist with resources, including by providing information on the assets of each National Laboratory that may enable the deployment and commercialization of technology.

children.heading: Training and education
children.label: D Training and education
children.text: The Foundation may support programs that provide training to researchers, scientists, other relevant personnel at National Laboratories and institutions of higher education, and previous or current recipients of or applicants for Department funding to help research, develop, demonstrate, deploy, and commercialize federally funded technology.

children.heading: Maturation funding
children.label: E Maturation funding
children.text: The Foundation shall support programs that provide maturation funding to researchers to advance the technology of those researchers for the purpose of moving products from a prototype stage to a commercial stage.

children.heading: Stakeholder engagement
children.label: F Stakeholder engagement
children.text: The Foundation shall convene, and may consult with, representatives from the Department, institutions of higher education, National Laboratories, the private sector, and commercialization organizations to develop programs for the purposes of the Foundation described in paragraph (3) and to advance the activities of the Foundation.

children.heading: Individual and Federal Laboratory-Associated Foundations
children.label: G Individual and Federal Laboratory-Associated Foundations
I
children.text: An Individual Laboratory-Associated Foundation.

children.label: II
children.text: A Federal Laboratory-Associated Foundation established pursuant to subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: Support
children.label: ii Support
children.text: The Foundation shall provide support to and collaborate with covered foundations.

children.label: I
children.text: a standard adaptable organizational design for responsible management;

children.label: II
children.text: standard and legally tenable bylaws and money-handling procedures; and

children.label: III
children.text: a standard training curriculum to orient and expand the operating expertise of personnel employed by covered foundations.

children.label: I
children.text: an existing Individual Laboratory-Associated Foundation to modify current practices or affiliate with the Foundation; or

children.label: II
children.text: a covered foundation to be bound by charter or corporate bylaws as permanently affiliated with the Foundation.

children.label: i
children.text: to conduct and support forums, meetings, conferences, courses, and training workshops consistent with the purposes of the Foundation described in paragraph (3);

children.label: ii
children.text: to support and encourage the understanding and development of data that promotes the translation of technologies from the research stage, through the development and maturation stage, and ending in the market stage;

children.label: iii
children.text: for writing, editing, printing, publishing, and vending books and other materials relating to research carried out under the Foundation and the Department; and

children.label: iv
children.text: to conduct other activities to carry out and support the purposes of the Foundation described in paragraph (3).

children.label: i
children.text: consist of qualitative and quantitative metrics; and

children.label: ii
children.text: include periodic third party evaluation of those programs and other activities of the Foundation.

children.heading: Communications
children.label: J Communications
children.text: The Foundation shall develop an expertise in communications to promote the work of grant and fellowship recipients under subparagraph (B), the commercialization successes of the Foundation, opportunities for partnership with the Foundation, and other activities.

children.heading: Solicitation and use of funds
children.label: K Solicitation and use of funds
children.text: The Foundation may solicit and accept gifts, grants, and other donations, establish accounts, and invest and expend funds in support of the activities and programs of the Foundation.

children.heading: Authority of the foundation
children.label: L Authority of the foundation
children.text: The Foundation shall be the sole entity responsible for carrying out the activities described in this paragraph.

children.heading: Executive Director
children.label: A Executive Director
children.text: The Board shall hire an Executive Director of the Foundation, who shall serve at the pleasure of the Board. Subject to the compliance with the policies and bylaws established pursuant to paragraph (2)(G), the Executive Director shall be responsible for the daily operations of the Foundation in carrying the activities described in paragraph (4).

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: B Compensation
children.text: The rate of compensation of the Executive Director shall be fixed by the Board.

children.heading: Administrative control
children.label: C Administrative control
children.text: No member of the Board, officer or employee of the Foundation or of any program established by the Foundation, or participant in a program established by the Foundation, shall exercise administrative control over any Federal employee.

children.label: i
children.text: a plan for the Foundation to become financially self-sustaining in fiscal year 2023 and thereafter (except for the amounts provided each fiscal year under paragraph (11)(A)(iii));

children.label: ii
children.text: a forecast of major crosscutting energy challenge opportunities, including short- and long-term objectives, identified by the Board, with input from communities representing the entities and areas of subject matter expertise, as applicable, described in paragraph (2)(B)(iii)(II);

children.label: I
children.text: grant awards, including selection, review, and notification;

children.label: II
children.text: communication of past, current, and future research priorities; and

children.label: III
children.text: solicitation of and response to public input on the opportunities identified under clause (ii);

children.label: iv
children.text: a description of the financial goals and benchmarks of the Foundation for the following 10 years;

children.label: v
children.text: a description of the efforts undertaken by the Foundation to engage historically underrepresented groups or regions, including through collaborations with historically Black colleges and universities, Tribal Colleges or Universities, minority-serving institutions, and minority-owned and women-owned business, and;2So in original. Probably should be “; and”.2

children.label: vi
children.text: a description of the efforts undertaken by the Foundation to ensure maximum complementarity and minimum redundancy with investments made by the Department.

children.label: i
children.text: describes the activities of the Foundation and the progress of the Foundation in furthering the purposes of the Foundation described in paragraph (3);

children.label: ii
children.text: provides a specific accounting of the source and use of all funds made available to the Foundation to carry out those activities to ensure transparency in the alignment of Department missions and policies with national security;

children.label: iii
children.text: describes how the results of the activities of the Foundation could be incorporated into the procurement processes of the General Services Administration; and

children.label: iv
children.text: includes a summary of each evaluation conducted using the evaluation methodology described in paragraph (4)(I).

children.label: I
children.text: the extent to which the Foundation is achieving the mission of the Foundation; and

children.label: II
children.text: the operation of the Foundation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any recommendations on how the Foundation may be improved.

children.label: i
children.text: provide for annual audits of the financial condition of the Foundation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: make the audits, and all other records, documents, and papers of the Foundation, available to the Secretary and the Comptroller General of the United States for examination or audit.

children.heading: Separate fund accounts
children.label: H Separate fund accounts
children.text: The Board shall ensure that any funds received under paragraph (11)(A) are held in a separate account from any other funds received by the Foundation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: To ensure integrity in the operations of the Foundation, the Board shall develop and enforce procedures relating to standards of conduct, financial disclosure statements, conflicts of interest (including recusal and waiver rules), audits, and any other matters determined appropriate by the Board.

children.label: I
children.text: the individual;

children.label: II
children.text: a relative (as defined in section 109 of the Ethics in Government Act of 1978 (5 U.S.C. App.) ) of that individual; or4See References in Text note below.4

children.label: III
children.text: a business organization or other entity in which the individual has an interest, including an organization or other entity with which the individual is negotiating employment.

children.heading: Intellectual property
children.label: J Intellectual property
children.text: The Board shall adopt written standards to govern the ownership and licensing of any intellectual property rights developed by the Foundation or derived from the collaborative efforts of the Foundation.

children.label: I
children.text: the Foundation;

children.label: II
children.text: a Federal entity with respect to an agreement of that Federal entity with the Foundation; or

children.label: III
children.text: an Individual Laboratory-Associated Foundation with respect to an agreement of that Federal entity with the Foundation.

children.heading: Full faith and credit
children.label: ii Full faith and credit
children.text: The full faith and credit of the United States shall not extend to any obligations of the Foundation.

children.heading: Nonapplicability of FACA
children.label: L Nonapplicability of FACA
children.text: The Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.) shall not apply to the Foundation or an Individual Laboratory-Associated Foundation.4

children.label: I
children.text: streamlining the ability of the Foundation to transfer equipment and funds to National Laboratories;

children.label: II
children.text: standardizing contract mechanisms to be used by the Foundation in engaging with National Laboratories; and

children.label: III
children.text: streamlining the ability of the Foundation to fund endowed positions at National Laboratories;

children.label: I
children.text: to accept and perform work for the Foundation, consistent with provided resources, notwithstanding any other provision of law governing the administration, mission, use, or operations of the National Laboratory or site, as applicable; and

children.label: II
children.text: to perform that work on a basis equal to other missions at the National Laboratory; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to permit the director of any National Laboratory or site of a National Laboratory to enter into a cooperative research and development agreement or negotiate a licensing agreement with the Foundation pursuant to .section 3710a of title 15

children.heading: Department liaisons
children.label: B Department liaisons
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint liaisons from across the Department to collaborate and coordinate with the Foundation, including not less than 1 liaison from the Office of Technology Transitions, who shall ensure that the Foundation works in conjunction with and does not duplicate existing activities and programs carried out by the Department, including the Technology Commercialization Fund of the Department.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: C Administration
children.text: The Secretary shall leverage appropriate arrangements, contracts, and directives to carry out the process developed under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: National security
children.label: 7 National security
children.text: Nothing in this subsection exempts the Foundation from any national security policy of the Department.

children.heading: Support services
children.label: 8 Support services
children.text: The Secretary may provide facilities, utilities, and support services to the Foundation if it is determined by the Secretary to be advantageous to the research programs of the Department.

children.heading: Preemption of authority
children.label: 9 Preemption of authority
children.text: This subsection shall not preempt any authority or responsibility of the Secretary under any other provision of law.

children.heading: Transfer funds
children.label: 10 Transfer funds
children.text: The Foundation may transfer funds to the Department, which shall be subject to all applicable Federal limitations relating to federally funded research.

children.label: i
children.text: not less than $1,500,000 shall be for the Secretary for fiscal year 2023 to establish the Foundation;

children.label: ii
children.text: not less than $30,000,000 shall be for the Foundation for fiscal year 2024 to carry out the activities of the Foundation; and

children.label: iii
children.text: not less than $3,000,000 shall be for the Foundation for each of the fiscal years 2025 through 2027 for administrative and operational costs.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: None of the funds authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary by subparagraph (A)(i) of this paragraph shall be used for construction.

children.heading: Cost share
children.label: C Cost share
children.text: Funds made available under subparagraph (A)(ii) shall be required to be cost-shared by a partner of the Foundation other than the Department or a National Laboratory.

children.heading: National Energy Technology Laboratory-Associated Foundation
children.label: c National Energy Technology Laboratory-Associated Foundation

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The National Energy Technology Laboratory may establish, or enter into an agreement with a nonprofit organization to establish, a Federal Laboratory-Associated Foundation (referred to in this subsection as a “Laboratory Foundation”) to support the mission of the National Energy Technology Laboratory.

children.heading: Not agency or instrumentality
children.label: B Not agency or instrumentality
children.text: A Laboratory Foundation shall not be an agency or instrumentality of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Governance structure
children.label: C Governance structure
children.text: A Laboratory Foundation established under subparagraph (A) shall have a separate governance structure from, and shall be managed independently of, the National Energy Technology Laboratory.

children.label: A
children.text: conducting support studies, competitions, projects, research, and other activities that further the purpose of the Laboratory Foundation;

children.label: B
children.text: carrying out programs to foster collaboration and partnership among researchers from the Federal Government, State governments, institutions of higher education, federally funded research and development centers, and industry and nonprofit organizations relating to the research, development, and commercialization of federally supported technologies;

children.label: C
children.text: carrying out programs to leverage technologies to support new product development that supports regional economic development;

children.label: i
children.text: to accelerate private sector competition and investment; and

children.label: ii
children.text: that complement the use of prize authority by the Department;

children.label: E
children.text: providing fellowships and grants to research and development personnel at, or affiliated with, federally funded centers, in accordance with paragraph (3);

children.label: i
children.text: that allow scientists from foreign countries to serve in research capacities in the United States or other countries in association with the National Energy Technology Laboratory;

children.label: ii
children.text: that provide opportunities for employees of the National Energy Technology Laboratory to serve in research capacities in foreign countries;

children.label: iii
children.text: to conduct studies, projects, or research in collaboration with national and international nonprofit and for-profit organizations, which may include the provision of stipends, travel, and other support for personnel;

children.label: I
children.text: to hold forums, meetings, conferences, courses, and training workshops that may include undergraduate, graduate, post-graduate, and post-doctoral accredited courses; and

children.label: II
children.text: for the accreditation of those courses by the Laboratory Foundation at the State and national level for college degrees or continuing education credits;

children.label: v
children.text: to support and encourage teachers and students of science at all levels of education;

children.label: vi
children.text: to promote an understanding of science amongst the general public;

children.label: vii
children.text: for writing, editing, printing, publishing, and vending of relevant books and other materials; and

children.label: viii
children.text: for the conduct of other activities to carry out and support the purpose of the Laboratory Foundation; and

children.label: G
children.text: receiving, administering, soliciting, accepting, and using funds, gifts, devises, or bequests, either absolutely or in trust of real or personal property or any income therefrom, or other interest or equity therein for the benefit of, or in connection with, the mission of the applicable Federal laboratory, in accordance with paragraph (4).

children.label: i
children.text: by a Laboratory Foundation and the donors to a Laboratory Foundation;

children.label: ii
children.text: subject to the agreement of the head of the agency the mission of which is supported by a Laboratory Foundation; and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a fellowship, based on the recommendation of the employees of the National Energy Technology Laboratory at which the fellow would serve.

children.heading: Expenses
children.label: B Expenses
children.text: Fellowships and grants described in paragraph (2)(E) may include stipends, travel, health insurance, benefits, and other appropriate expenses.

children.heading: Gifts
children.label: 4 Gifts
children.text: An amount of funds, a gift, a devise, or a bequest described in paragraph (2)(G) may be accepted by a Laboratory Foundation regardless of whether it is encumbered, restricted, or subject to a beneficial interest of a private person if any current or future interest of the funds, gift, devise, or bequest is for the benefit of the research and development activities of the National Energy Technology Laboratory.

children.heading: Ownership by Federal Government
children.label: 5 Ownership by Federal Government
children.text: A contribution, gift, or any other transfer made to or for the use of a Laboratory Foundation shall be regarded as a contribution, gift, or transfer to or for the use of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Liability
children.label: 6 Liability
children.text: The United States shall not be liable for any debts, defaults, acts, or omissions of a Laboratory Foundation.

children.heading: Transfer of funds
children.label: 7 Transfer of funds
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a Laboratory Foundation may transfer funds to the National Energy Technology Laboratory and the National Energy Technology Laboratory may accept that transfer of funds.

children.heading: Other laws
children.label: 8 Other laws
children.text: This subsection shall not alter or supersede any other provision of law governing the authority, scope, establishment, or use of nonprofit organizations by a Federal agency.
